title: Optimized Binary Search with Incorrect Handling of Duplicate Elements

statement.markdown: Implement a binary search function that searches for a target element in a sorted array. The function should return the index of the target element if it exists, or -1 if it does not exist. However, the current implementation has a logical bug when dealing with duplicate elements. The bug causes the function to return incorrect results when there are duplicate elements in the array. Fix this bug to ensure the function works correctly for duplicate elements as well.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a sorted array of integers and a target integer.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be the index of the target element if it exists, or -1 if it does not exist.
statement.notes: The function should use binary search to achieve an efficient time complexity of O(log n).

constraints.description: The size of the input array.
The target element to search for.

examples.explanation: The target element 5 is present in the array at index 2.
The target element 2 is not present in the array.
The target element 6 is not present in the array.

hints.text: Review the binary search algorithm and its time complexity.

editorial.title: Optimized Binary Search with Duplicate Elements
editorial.intuition: To handle duplicate elements, we need to keep track of the first occurrence of the target element.
editorial.bruteForce: A naive approach would be to perform a linear search, but this would result in a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a modified binary search algorithm that keeps track of the first occurrence of the target element.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a binary search algorithm that handles duplicate elements by keeping track of the first occurrence of the target element.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the algorithm can be proven by showing that it returns the correct index for the target element in all cases.
editorial.dryRun: To understand the algorithm better, let's perform a dry run with the example input.
commonMistakes: Not using binary search for efficiency

functionSignature.cpp: void binary_search(int* arr, int n, int target);
functionSignature.java: public int binarySearch(int[] arr, int n, int target);
functionSignature.python: def binary_search(self, arr, n, target: int) -> int:
